titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Hindsight 2020: New Haven Project Collection
titlestmt.author: compiled by Michelle Peralta

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.
This collection contains 15 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 208 digital files.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday July 6, 2023 at 08:39

descriptivenote.p: The materials are in English, Spanish, and Arabic.

did.abstract: The collection comprises submissions to the Yale Manuscripts and Archives' Hindsight 2020: New Haven project documenting life in the Greater New Haven area during the events of 2020 and their continuing impact.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Hindsight 2020: New Haven Project Collection (MS 2125). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection comprises submissions collected by Yale Library Manuscripts and Archives' Hindsight 2020: New Haven project documenting life in the Greater New Haven area during the events of 2020 and their continuing impact. Included are 10 born-digital submissions submitted through the Hindsight 2020 website, 93 postcards collected at events on the New Haven Green and through stations at local public libraries, digital surrogates of the postcards, and a capture of the Hindsight 2020 website.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in the collection are arranged by submission type and chronologically therein.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.
Original computer files may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult access copies.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright for materials submitted to the Hindsight 2020: New Haven project is retained by their creators. Copyright for content created by the project coordinators is held by Yale University.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Hindsight 2020: New Haven project was launched in fall 2020 by Yale Manuscripts and Archive's staff Michelle Peralta and Jennifer Coggins with the goals of engaging the community in collaborative memory work and preserving a representative record of 2020 and its ongoing effects on New Haven. Residents contributed reflections via an online platform and through postcard stations on the New Haven Green and at local public libraries. These contributions were shared on the project website through summer 2023. Submissions to the project closed in January 2022.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Manuscripts and Archives staff arranged and described these materials according to national and local standards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dates for the materials were determined by the date of submission to the project website.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at beinecke.library@yale.edu to request access.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This material was imaged or captured by Manuscripts and Archives staff in 2022 for preservation and access. Additional information about actions taken may be available at the repository.

did.unittitle: Instructions for postcard stations at public libraries
International Festival of Arts and Ideas

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at beinecke.library@yale.edu to request access.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The date for the material was determined by date of capture.
